title: Lyonia fasciculata
intro: Lyonia fasciculata, also known as Stag Holly, is a shrub plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its spiny, evergreen foliage and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, as well as support overall cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Lyonia fasciculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations of Lyonia fasciculata, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used in traditional medicine to support health and wellness.
taxonomy: Lyonia fasciculata is a shrub in the heath family, Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Lyonia, a group of evergreen shrubs native to North America. The classification of Lyonia fasciculata is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Ericales, Family: Ericaceae, Genus: Lyonia, Species: L. fasciculata. This classification highlights its relationship to other plants within the Ericaceae family. The classification of Lyonia fasciculata provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Lyonia fasciculata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly by indigenous communities in North America. The plant's bark and roots are often utilized for their purported medicinal properties. Some reports suggest that Lyonia fasciculata may be used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, more research is necessary to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. Traditional use of Lyonia fasciculata highlights the importance of preserving and respecting indigenous knowledge and cultural practices.

parts: Lyonia fasciculata, also known as the fawn's breath or Appalachian fawn's breath, is a shrub native to the eastern United States.
